heading: Receipts from sale of used or surplus furniture and furnishings of Senate
label: 6517 Receipts from sale of used or surplus furniture and furnishings of Senate
text: On and after , receipts from the sale of used or surplus furniture and furnishings shall be deposited in the United States Treasury for credit to the appropriation for “Senate Office Buildings” under the heading “Architect of the Capitol.”October 1, 1982
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section, and to section 170a of former Title 40, prior to the enactment of Title 40, Public Buildings, Property, and Works, by , , .section 117b–1 of this titlePub. L. 107–217, § 1116 Stat. 1062Aug. 21, 2002Section is based on title I (2d proviso under “Senate Office Buildings”) of S. 2939, as reported , which was enacted into law by .Sept. 22, 1982Pub.
